items.title: [Atlas Obscura] Hoffmann Lime Kilns in Langcliffe, England
items.content_html:
🎨 🌾 🔧 🪶 🌲 🏔️ 🔭 ✏️

This is a fascinating Victorian feature of the Yorkshire Dales National Park, which details the history of lime production in the area and includes various ruined structures, such as the Hoffmann Kiln from 1873. This is regarded as the best preserved example of this building in England and was operational until 1931. The site was constructed next to the Settle-Carlisle railway for ease of transportation and now houses a modern business park and archaeology trail at the base of Stainforth Scar.

items.title: [AI Feed Curator] State of the Feed — Week of July 6–12, 2026
items.content_html:
During the week of July 6–12, the aggregator completed 8 runs and processed 7,488 total articles, delivering 432 quality articles for curation. The news category dominated intake at 200 articles, followed by ai-tech and wellness each at 52 articles, with smaller contributions from science (40), local (38), climate (28), and homelab (19). The dimensional scoring model weighted relevance most heavily at 0.55, with quality and local factors at 0.25 and 0.20 respectively. Pipeline efficiency remained stable with a mean noise-to-signal ratio of 2.09 across seven measured runs, indicating consistent filtering performance within the expected range of 1.23–2.76.

Feed health showed degradation in five sources: Kagi Summarizer, OpenMedia, Daniele Messi — Writing, First Peoples Cultural Council (Kagi), and Nation Talk (Kagi) all reported errors this week. Two new feeds were integrated into the pipeline: Tŝilhqot'in National Government and EBSCO. Feed discovery identified Bambu Lab Blog as a top candidate for addition, scoring 61.7 on the ranking algorithm and aligning with existing homelab content priorities.

Calibration

KnobOldNewRationale
limits.min_claude_score1316User feedback shows current min_claude_score=13 is far too permissive (only cutting 18.8% of bad articles). Threshold_sweep indicates 20 would cut 28.1% of bad at 5.7% good lost, but that's the max bound. Proposing 16 as a conservative first step: the June 24 reset went from 20→13 to fix feed scarcity, but final_feeds now show 295-398/run, well above the ~100 target. Raising to 16 tightens quality without risking the overshoot that triggered the manual intervention. This will reduce noise-to-signal by cutting more articles at the quality gate.
limits.haiku_scrub_floor1013The Haiku safety check currently reviews articles scoring 10+. User feedback shows 63.3% bad rate across all content, and the 0-19 composite band is 87.6% bad. Raising haiku_scrub_floor from 10→13 subjects more borderline articles to the safety check, which should catch additional fluff/sponsored content before it reaches the quality gate. This complements the min_claude_score raise and targets the same quality issue from a different stage of the pipeline.

items.title: [MacRumors] Apple's Manufacturing in India Gets Boost From New Tariff Exemptions
items.content_html:
🔧 🪶 ✏️

🔧 ✏️

🔧 ✏️

🔧 ✏️

Apple stands to gain from India's decision to eliminate import duties on a range of components used in smartphone manufacturing, in a move that could further lower costs for the company's rapidly growing India-based supply chain.


According to a report from Reuters, the Indian government has done away with tariffs of 7.5% and 5% that had applied to inputs for wireless charging hardware, automotive and medical device screens, and lithium-ion battery cells. The exemptions are set to remain in effect through to March 31, 2029.

The wireless charging component exemption, in particular, feeds directly into the MagSafe ecosystem used across the iPhone lineup. With import costs on that hardware now removed, Apple's India-based assembly partners have a clearer path to sourcing and building charging components domestically rather than importing them at a markup.

Apple has leaned heavily on India as it works to shift iPhone production away from China, with assembly partners now building roughly a quarter of all iPhones in the country and producing the entire iPhone 17 lineup there for the first time, including the higher-end Pro and Pro Max models. Foxconn, one of Apple's main assemblers, poured $1.5 billion into expanding its India operations earlier this year, and Tata Electronics has grown into an equally central manufacturing partner alongside it.
Tags: India, MagSafe

This article, "Apple's Manufacturing in India Gets Boost From New Tariff Exemptions" first appeared on MacRumors.com
